final_response: Logged as a decision: decisions.md#2026-05-17-postgres-for-events-service (datastore commitment constrains future work). Also dropped a pointer line in today's daybook Log.

actions.summary: Appended new decision entry: 2026-05-17 — Postgres for events service (Status: accepted).
Created today's daybook with a Log pointer to the new decision entry.

notes: Routing call: user committed to a datastore (Postgres) for a new service. That is a commitment that constrains future action — schema, migrations, client libs all target Postgres going forward — so it clears the strict bar for decisions.md rather than observations.md or just a daybook line.
User's 'Log that' is a directive to record durably; decisions.md is the durable commitment surface. Added a daybook Log line as a chronological pointer so the day's record reflects the routing.
Used heading slug convention from AGENTS.md (`## 2026-05-17 — Short title`) so the GitHub anchor is stable and matches the cross-reference form in the daybook.

Did not invent a project folder — only one fact was given (datastore choice). If/when the events service grows scope, that's the time to spin up `projects/events-service/` and link back to this decision.
